---
title: "Water Audit Resources"
format: html
---

# 💧 Water Audit Resources: Accessing Regional Support

Goal: To connect congregations with local utility programs that provide free or subsidized water audits, equipment rebates, and infrastructure funding. 

-----

## ️🌵 Regional Program Directory

Several major Texas utilities offer specific programs to help non-profits and commercial customers identify waste and offset the cost of high-efficiency upgrades. 

### [Houston Public Works](https://www.houstonpublicworks.org/smart-utility-check-up) 

Houston offers comprehensive tools for its commercial and institutional partners to manage water demand. 

  - **Free Audit Tools:** Access the Smart Utility Check Up and the Goldwater Dashboard Planning Tool to monitor and manage facility water use. 
  - **Direct Support:** The water conservation team provides presentations and resources specifically for churches and organizations. 
  - **Contact:** Email WaterConservation@houstontx.gov. 

### [Austin Water](https://www.austintexas.gov/page/commercial-multifamily-schools-rebates#Commercial) 

Austin provides one of the most robust financial incentive packages in the state for houses of worship and non-profits. 

  - **Water Efficiency Audit Rebate:** Austin Water covers 75% of the cost of a professional water audit, up to a maximum of $5,000. 
  - **Requirement:** The facility must be separately metered and use at least 100,000 gallons of water annually. 
  - **"Bucks for Business":** Incentive programs for large-scale equipment retrofits, such as cooling tower upgrades or process water reuse. 
  - **Free Tools:** Free high-efficiency faucet aerators and showerheads are often available for qualifying multi-family or school-related facilities. 

### [Fort Worth Water](https://www.fortworthtexas.gov/departments/water/savefwwater) 

Fort Worth emphasizes education and direct hardware replacement through its "Smart" program suite. 

  - **SmartFlush Commercial:** Congregations can receive vouchers for up to 10 free low-flow toilets (additional units may be requested after inspection). 
  - **Smart Irrigation Evaluation:** Free professional evaluations of your irrigation system to identify leaks, broken heads, and inefficient controller settings. 
  - **Business Smart:** A commercial-focused assessment program to reduce a facility's overall water footprint. 

### [LCRA Territory (Lower Colorado River Authority)](https://www.lcra.org/water/watersmart/commercial-rebates/) 

Congregations in LCRA's service area (including many in the Hill Country and Central Texas) have access to significant "WaterSmart" funding. 

  - **Audit Rebates:** LCRA provides rebates of up to $125 per hour for professional indoor/outdoor water audits, with a cap of $5,000. 
  - **Project Funding:** Receive a rebate for 50% of the cost (up to $20,000) for installing water-saving technologies like rainwater harvesting or cooling tower recycling. 
  - **Fixture Incentives:** Standard rebates cover 50% of the cost for high-efficiency toilets ($100/unit), soil moisture sensors ($900), and smart controllers ($100). 

-----

## 🛑 Summary of Financial Opportunities 

| Region | Audit Support | Major Rebates |
| :-: | :-: | :-: |
| **Austin** | 75% of cost up to $5,000 | Custom "Bucks for Business" incentives |
| **Houston** | Free digital tools & dashboards | Water conservation items & credits |
| **Fort Worth** | Free Smart Irrigation evaluations | 10+ Free Toilets via SmartFlush |
| **LCRA** | Up to $125/hour ($5k cap) | $20,000 cap for custom tech |



-----

### Getting Started 

Before applying for these programs, ensure you have 24 months of utility bills ready and have identified all water meters on your property.  Most rebates require pre-approval before work begins.
